headline: OpenAI weighs leasing Ohio data center with Nvidia backing, The Information reports
summary: OpenAI is in talks to lease a proposed 10-gigawatt data center campus on federal ‌land in Ohio, in a deal that could ‌include financial backing from Nvidia, The Information reported on Tuesday, citing two ​people with direct knowledge of the discussions.  • Nvidia is expected to supply hardware in the facility and provide a financial guarantee for OpenAI's lease and SB Energy's financing, according to the report.  OpenAI and Nvidia ​did not immediately respond to Reuters' ​requests for comment outside ​regular business hours.

content.title: OpenAI weighs leasing Ohio data center with Nvidia backing, The Information reports
content.summary: OpenAI is in talks to lease a proposed 10-gigawatt data center campus on federal ‌land in Ohio, in a deal that could ‌include financial backing from Nvidia, The Information reported on Tuesday, citing two ​people with direct knowledge of the discussions.  • Nvidia is expected to supply hardware in the facility and provide a financial guarantee for OpenAI's lease and SB Energy's financing, according to the report.  OpenAI and Nvidia ​did not immediately respond to Reuters' ​requests for comment outside ​regular business hours.

headline: Is Oracle's Q4 earnings the next big test for AI trade?
summary: The AI trade continues to stand on unsteady ground this week as semiconductor stocks waver. Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal fourth quarter earnings are due out after Wednesday's market close, which could ultimately be a tipping point for the AI sector. JonesTrading chief market strategist Michael O'Rourke speaks with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi about his outlook on the AI sector.

content.title: Is Oracle's Q4 earnings the next big test for AI trade?
content.description:
The AI trade continues to stand on unsteady ground this week as semiconductor stocks waver. Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal fourth quarter earnings are due out after Wednesday's market close, which could ultimately be a tipping point for the AI sector.

JonesTrading chief market strategist Michael O'Rourke speaks with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi about his outlook on the AI sector.

headline: South Korea's SK Hynix eyes US listing as soon as August, sources say
summary:
STORY: SK Hynix reportedly plans to list its shares in the U.S. as soon as August.

That's according to two sources familiar with the matter...

The South Korean memory chipmaker wants to capitalise on strong appetite for AI-linked stocks and broaden its investor base.

One source added the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is likely to approve SK Hynix's American listing application during the week of June 22nd.

SK Hynix said in March it had confidentially filed for a U.S. listing.

A source said at the time the offering could raise as much as $14 billion.

The company is the world's second-largest memory chipmaker and a key supplier to Nvidia.

It's scored big in the AI boom due to its dominant position in high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI servers.

Its share price has jumped 240% this year and its market value has topped $1 trillion since May.

That makes it only the third Asian company after Taiwan's TSMC and Samsung Electronics to hit the milestone.

An August debut would also add another top name to what is shaping up to be a busy second half for U.S. equity markets.

content.title: South Korea's SK Hynix eyes US listing as soon as August, sources say
content.summary:
STORY: SK Hynix reportedly plans to list its shares in the U.S. as soon as August.

That's according to two sources familiar with the matter...

The South Korean memory chipmaker wants to capitalise on strong appetite for AI-linked stocks and broaden its investor base.

One source added the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is likely to approve SK Hynix's American listing application during the week of June 22nd.

SK Hynix said in March it had confidentially filed for a U.S. listing.

A source said at the time the offering could raise as much as $14 billion.

The company is the world's second-largest memory chipmaker and a key supplier to Nvidia.

It's scored big in the AI boom due to its dominant position in high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI servers.

Its share price has jumped 240% this year and its market value has topped $1 trillion since May.

That makes it only the third Asian company after Taiwan's TSMC and Samsung Electronics to hit the milestone.

An August debut would also add another top name to what is shaping up to be a busy second half for U.S. equity markets.

headline: OpenAI Reportedly Nears Deal for Massive Ohio Data Centre Project with Nvidia Support
summary: OpenAI (NASDAQ:OAI) is reportedly in advanced discussions to secure a long-term lease for a proposed 10-gigawatt data centre campus in Ohio, according to a report from The Information. Nvidia is said to be involved in supporting the project, which could become one of the largest artificial intelligence infrastructure developments ever undertaken.
